notable_excerpts: At the end of this quarter, our renewable energy capacity was at 418 Megawatts, powered by solar, wind, and hydel resources.
Our aluminum specific GHG footprint for the quarter was at 19.11 tons of CO2 per ton of aluminum produced, which is lower than the quarter period of the last fiscal year.
82% of the total waste generated was recycled or reused, indicating stronger waste management performance.
Our specific water consumption in aluminum has further decreased, driven by the installation of an RO ZLD Plant and Tube settler.
We remain deeply committed to preserving and enhancing our biodiversity in and around the areas of operation.
At Hindalco, safety is always our highest priority. Our LTIFR for this quarter is at 0.22, showing significant improvement over the prior period.
We regret to report a road safety incident that resulted in a fatality at one of our Indian operations.
These projects are designed to deliver measurable ecological benefits while empowering local communities as stewards of restoration.
Hindalco has scored 89 out of 100 in the S&P Global CSA 2025, the highest ever score achieved by the company.
We are implementing measures to prevent man and machine interface risks across all our manufacturing units.
Our strategic priorities are clearly defined, accelerating capacity expansion across the aluminum and copper upstream businesses.

chain_of_thought: Hindalco Industries demonstrates strong environmental initiatives with significant renewable energy capacity expansion and waste management improvements. Social aspects show commitment to safety and community engagement, though marred by a safety incident. Governance is robust with high transparency and strategic risk management, reflected in high S&P Global CSA scores.
weaknesses: Limited specific details on diversity and inclusion
red_flags: Fatality in a road safety incident
